title: platform done verify script L2+
purpose: D6.2 defines platform done verify script l2 for the agent-driven expert system. Platform evolution — promotion ladder, parallel queue, reuse.
scope: Owns `D6.2` only; siblings under `D6` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §6 — Branch D — Platform evolution plane (parallel queue)](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#6-branch-d-platform-evolution-plane-parallel-queue).
behavior_steps: For L2 and above promotions, definition of done requires a verify path—unit test, verify-router command, or validate-workflow hook—that proves the script or tool behaves as specified.
Script promotion pairs [D4.2](D4.2-platform-work-script-extraction.md) extraction with a test file in `tests/unit/` before the conductor dequeues the platform item.
L1 playbooks may cite manual verify until scripted; L2+ without automated verify fails platform done and blocks [D2.3](D2.3-dequeue-platform-turn-not-product.md) head advance.
Verifier writes platform-appropriate logs under `evidence/` or captures unit test output; conductor dual-writes `last_verify` before marking promotion done.
False done without verify triggers audit re-enqueue and pursuit stops at H2 until automated proof exists.

narrative: D6.2 defines platform done verify script l2 for the agent-driven expert system. Platform evolution — promotion ladder, parallel queue, reuse. In practice, the system for l2 and above promotions, definition of done requires a verify path—unit test, verify-router command, or validate-workflow hook—that proves the script or tool behaves as specified, then script promotion pairs [d4.2](d4.2-platform-work-script-extraction.md) extraction with a test file in `tests/unit/` before the conductor dequeues the platform item.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-d 2026-06-28 -->

For L2 and above promotions, definition of done requires a verify path—unit test, verify-router command, or validate-workflow hook—that proves the script or tool behaves as specified. L1 playbooks may cite manual verify until scripted; L2+ without automated verify fails platform done.

Evidence discipline mirrors product tasks but writes platform-appropriate logs under `evidence/` or unit test output. Script promotion pairs extraction ([D4.2](D4.2-platform-work-script-extraction.md)) with test file in `tests/unit/`. False done without verify triggers audit re-enqueue and blocks [D2.3](D2.3-dequeue-platform-turn-not-product.md) head advance.
